title: NGOs sue French state over failure to tackle 'forever chemicals'
subhed: NGOs and residents living near contaminated sites filed legal action against the state to remedy the 'ecological harm' caused by its 'culpable inaction.'

'Gaza has become a diplomatic, political and military black hole'
subhed: Donald Trump's promises for the territory, devastated after two years of war, have continued to be proven wrong in the months following the ceasefire with Israel, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

title: Trump tightens grip on Republican Party in US primaries
subhed: Candidates backed by Donald Trump for the Republican Party nomination in the November midterm elections benefit from the mobilization of the MAGA base, which remains loyal to the billionaire despite his declining popularity.

title: These winemakers believe melody makes better wine: 'Right after bottling, we play Beethoven on a loop'
subhed: In the vineyards, while fermenting or even during aging, some winemakers use various melodies that they believe stimulate the vines, protect them from disease or intensify the wine's aromas.

title: JR covers Pont-Neuf bridge in Paris with giant inflatable cave
subhed: The temporary installation 'La Caverne du Pont-Neuf' has been set up on the Paris landmark since Thursday. The site will be open to the public free of charge, 24 hours a day, for three weeks from June 6.

'Team Musul': The French youths convicted of attempting to join IS in Mozambique
subhed: Six young French nationals were convicted in Paris for planning to migrate to Mozambique together and join the IS-affiliated jihadist group al-Shabab in Cabo Delgado province, where it has been active since 2017.

title: ShinyHunters, the elusive hacking network that started in France
subhed: Despite multiple arrests, numerous data breaches continue to be claimed under the ShinyHunters banner. Beyond its French roots, the group has now become a cybercriminal 'brand.'

title: Gaza's vanished past lives on through photographs accidentally rediscovered in Marseille
subhed: Kegham Djeghalian, a photographer and Armenian genocide survivor, settled in Gaza in 1944. He documented the Palestinian territory through portraits and reports until he died in 1981. His grandson has chosen to exhibit his images of ordinary and joyful moments in Gaza, now devastated by two years of war.
